title: Does absolute evil exist? Why is there suffering in this world? How does free will distinguish human beings? If Allah’s mercy supersedes His punishment, why is life rife with trials? Addressing these questions is at the core of understanding Allah’s magnificence and the world around us. Join us for this academic mini-series as we delve into reflection and conversation on some of the essential questions of life. We hope to see you there!

title: Assalamu Alaikum - Suhba April Event!
description: 20 likes, 0 comments - nbic_pics on April 16, 2026: "Assalamu Alaikum - Suhba April Event! Join Imam Ali Ben Saad on *Wednesday, April 29th at 7:15pm* as he indulges in dialogue on spiritual reenactment for our monthly event: *Spring into Action - Spiritual Renewal Post-Ramadan* Ramadan is the month of spiritual renewal and ‘highs’, but how can we best ensure that these practices are continued when the month comes to an end? Imam Ali will be focusing on practical ways to always remain connected to our deen through engaging conversations Chai and light refreshment will be served, please RSVP using the link below! Inshallah we will see you all there. https://forms.gle/aGGLqgQ4v9eSRTBH7".

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Join us for Fajr Suhba!
description: Join us for Fajr Suhba! Every Sunday after Fajr Salah at Al Falah Center. Pray together, reflect on Qur’an & Sunnah, enjoy breakfast, and strengthen our community bonds. Bring your family and renew your faith in an uplifting environment!

title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.

The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. title: Lessons From Surah Yusuf
description: Join us for Friday Halaqa after Isha, as we reflect on lessons from Surah Yusuf. We will explore patience during hardship, trust in Allah during uncertainty, integrity under pressure, forgiveness over resentment, and how Allah’s plan unfolds over time. This session connects the story of Prophet Yusuf to real challenges you face today, including family conflict, temptation, setbacks, and leadership. All are welcome. Come to listen, reflect, and grow together.

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Join Shaykh Ismail Bowers for an intimate Eid al-Adha retreat through the spiritual heart of Morocco 🇲🇦
description: 114 likes, 0 comments - buraqtravel.co on February 1, 2026: "Join Shaykh Ismail Bowers for an intimate Eid al-Adha retreat through the spiritual heart of Morocco 🇲🇦 ✦ Fez — home to the world’s oldest university ✦ Meknes — where history and living heritage meet ✦ Ifrane — Eid prayers + celebration in the Atlas Mountains ✦ Casablanca — closing reflections at the iconic Hassan II Mosque 8 days. 50 seats. All-inclusive packages available. Whether you’re drawn by faith, fascinated by history, or simply hungry for something meaningful — this journey is for you. 📅 May 23–30, 2026 🔗 Link in bio or visit iscj.org/morocco #Morocco #EidAlAdha #ISCJ #BuraqTravel".

title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. While the spaces have been cleared, conditions remain less than ideal and parking capacity is limited. For safety reasons, we do not recommend full community attendance for Isha and Taraweeh at this time. Isha and a shortened 8 rakat Taraweeh will be conducted. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).

It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. title: 🚨 Update on Ihsan Academy’s Quran Camp Summer Program at ICPC-Clifton! 🚨
description: 5 likes, 0 comments - icpcnj on July 14, 2025: "🚨 Update on Ihsan Academy’s Quran Camp Summer Program at ICPC-Clifton! 🚨 Our Nourania level is now FULL — Jazakum Allahu Khairan to all who registered! 🌟 There are still limited spots left in the Intermediate and Advanced levels — perfect for students ready to strengthen their recitation and memorization. 📍 Location: ICPC-Clifton 📖 Don’t miss this chance to elevate your child’s connection with the Quran this summer. Since the program began on 7/7, you can now register at a reduced rate! 👉 Register now: www.icpcnj.org/summer2025 #ICPCClifton #IhsanAcademy #QuranCamp #SummerProgram #QuranForKids #IslamicEducation #CliftonNJ #LearnQuran #QuranJourney #ICPCYouth #Nourania #IntermediateQuran #AdvancedQuran #QuranGoals".

Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. title: Community Halaqa
description: 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM.
raw_text: Bayonne Muslims | Masjid and Community Center in Bayonne, NJ About Education As-Sirat Quran Academy Little Khalifas Academy Summer Camp Interact News Volunteer Social Media Notice to Parents Feedback Contact Us Donate SCHOOL Bayonne Muslims Prayer Times Salah Adhan Iqama Fajr 04:52 AM 05:12 AM Dhuhr 12:56 PM 01:30 PM Asr 04:42 PM 04:57 PM Magraib 07:40 PM 07:45 PM Isha 09:01 PM 09:16 PM Jummah 1 12:45PM 1:15PM Jummah 2 1:45PM 2:15PM Announcements Eid Al-Fitr Announcements Eid al-Fitr will be on Friday, March 20, 2026. 1. The community Eid al-Fitr prayer will be held at Stephen R Gregg Park this year. The prayer will be held next to Baseball Field 4 at 8:45 AM. Directions: https://maps.app.goo.gl/c5uCKUUvs4ufMvG58 There will NOT be any Eid prayers at 16th Street Park this year. 2. For anyone that can't attend the community prayer at the park, there will be an early Eid prayer held at the masjid (109 E 24th St) at 7:30 AM. 3. For this Friday, March 20th 2026, there will only be a single Jummah Khutbah at 1 PM. The masjid will return to the regular schedule of two Jummah Khutbahs next week. 19 Mar 2026 10:40 AM Jummah Times Jummah Prayer 1: 12:45PM Jummah Prayer 2: 1:45PM 12 Mar 2026 06:41 PM Welcoming Ramadan 2026 In accordance with AMJA and Bayonne Muslims global moon sighting policy, the moon was sighted and therefore InshaAllah the first day of Ramadan will be Wednesday, February 18th inshaAllah. We will pray taraweah tonight, Tuesday night. Taraweah/Isha at the masjid will start 7:30pm inshaAllah. Download the BayonneMuslims app for all prayer timings. Please come early and follow all masjid guidelines regarding parking and other safety precautions. title: Sunday School
description: The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful).
raw_text: Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Skip to content 📞 973-588-4059 📧 info@jmic.org Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Live Donate School Home Masjid Events Services Imam Appointment Nikkah Education Services Alphabetland Sunday School Quran Learning Institute Community Services Social Services JMIC Assembly Hall Literary Social Media YouTube Channel Facebook Instagram Our App Photo Gallery Donate Funeral Services Contact Welcome To Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center The Jam-e-Masjid Islamic Center Inc., was established in May, 1988 as a non-profit religious educational organization to serve the Muslim community. It is a community-based organization depending on its volunteers to enable the development of a model Islamic society based on the guidance from the Quran and the Sunnah of Prophet Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) to maximize our reverence towards Allah (most gracious most merciful). The dedicated volunteers work in harmony towards the community’s efforts to: Build and sustain a progressive religious and cultural center for fulfilling our spiritual and social responsibilities. Build strong and cohesive relationships within the community irrespective of ethnicity, race, and religion. Facilitate Islamic education for children and adults. Enhance peace and justice. Develop a culture of caring. Mission Statement Help meet the religious, social, and cultural needs of the Muslim community, via the Sunday School, Alphabet-land, Quranic School, Salat-ul-Jumuah, Al-Ramadan, and Eid Programs. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Join the Muslim Center of Greater Princeton on our journey to grow, empower, and serve our community Asalaamualaikum MCGP Family, Alhamdulillah, the MCGP team has worked diligently to clean the primary parking lot and 379 lot. While the spaces have been cleared, conditions remain less than ideal and parking capacity is limited. For safety reasons, we do not recommend full community attendance for Isha and Taraweeh at this time. Isha and a shortened 8 rakat Taraweeh will be conducted. We strongly encourage elders and children to remain at home. 🤲 Isha salaah will be at 8pm, inshallah This Ramadan, come pray, reflect, and grow together as one ummah. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. We are active members in political, interfaith and charity projects throughout the county and state. (We are currently constructing our outreach page. Join the Muslim Center of Greater Princeton on our journey to grow, empower, and serve our community Asalaamualaikum MCGP Family, Alhamdulillah, the MCGP team has worked diligently to clean the primary parking lot and 379 lot. While the spaces have been cleared, conditions remain less than ideal and parking capacity is limited. For safety reasons, we do not recommend full community attendance for Isha and Taraweeh at this time. Isha and a shortened 8 rakat Taraweeh will be conducted. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. Events Calender About the Masjid Established in 1986, THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF MONMOUTH COUNTY (ISMC) is a non-profit organization with the objective of strengthening the bond of humanity. We are dedicated to creating an atmosphere for better citizenship and improving the well being of our community at large. Serving all those in need is a primary goal for our organization. Through our association with several humanitarian relief and development organizations, we are able to respond to human suffering in emergency and disaster situations at home and abroad. Get to Know us Our Services The Islamic Society of Monmouth County is committed to making all services required by the Muslim community easily accessible including but not limited to: Marriage Services Allah says in Qur’an, “And of His signs is that He created for you from yourselves mates that you may find tranquillity in them; and He placed between you affection and mercy. Indeed in that are signs for a people who give thought.” 30:21 Youth Programs Our goal is to engage today’s youth with engaging activities that help with building our future generation. It is held in the first Saturday of each month between 7:00pm and 9:00pm. It’s open for everyone, no reservation required. ICUC Outreach is dedicated in exemplifying good muslims values by serving our community. As muslims we set the best example for others to follow. At ICUC Outreach we are involved in improving connections with members of our local community, muslim and non-muslim. title: Alhamdulillah 🌙✨
description: 169 likes, 3 comments - icpcnj on April 6, 2026: "Alhamdulillah 🌙✨ We were honored to host the Ramadan Qur’an Competition Awards Ceremony at ICPC Paterson on March 28th. It was a beautiful gathering celebrating dedication perseverance and love for the Book of Allah We were also blessed by the presence of our respected shuyookh whose attendance added honor and inspiration to the evening Congratulations to all of our participants and winners your hard work and commitment to the Qur’an are truly inspiring. May Allah continue to bless you increase you in knowledge and make the Qur’an the light of your hearts A heartfelt thank you to our organizers judges and families who supported this meaningful event #Ramadan2026 #QuranCompetition #ICPCPaterson #PeopleOfQuran #LoveForQuran".

title: Surah Maryam is a powerful and deeply moving surah that reveals how Allah supports His servants in their most difficult and lonely moments. Through its stories, we witness what it truly means to rely on Him, remain steadfast, and hold firmly to faith no matter the circumstances.
description: 22 likes, 0 comments - icpcnj on April 8, 2026: "Surah Maryam is a powerful and deeply moving surah that reveals how Allah supports His servants in their most difficult and lonely moments. Through its stories, we witness what it truly means to rely on Him, remain steadfast, and hold firmly to faith no matter the circumstances. This surah invites us to reflect on our relationship with Allah, how we turn to Him in hardship, and how certain we are in His promise. Join us for this Tafseer Series with Sh. Ismail Hamdi Wednesdays after Maghrib ICPC – Younis Register now: www.icpcnj.org/courses #suratmaryam #tafseer".
